message:
fix(test): update health endpoint tests for dev mode compatibility

- Handle /api/health/status returning 200 in development mode
  (endpoint is accessible without auth when JFP_HEALTH_STATUS_TOKEN
  is not set and NODE_ENV is not production)
- Add warmup requests before response time measurements to account
  for initial route compilation
- Use generous timeouts (5s) suitable for dev/CI environments
- All 16 health endpoint tests now pass

Closes: jeffreysprompts.com-3w6v

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3q8a","title":"Implement keyboard shortcuts for power users","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-4us. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Implement keyboard shortcuts for power users.

## Why This Matters
Power users expect keyboard navigation. Good keyboard shortcuts:
- Speed up common workflows
- Improve accessibility
- Create muscle memory for frequent actions

## Keyboard Shortcuts

### Global Shortcuts
| Shortcut | Action | Context |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `Cmd/Ctrl + K` | Open search | Global |
| `Cmd/Ctrl + /` | Show shortcuts help | Global |
| `Cmd/Ctrl + N` | Create new prompt | Global |
| `Escape` | Close modal/panel | When open |
| `G then H` | Go to home/dashboard | Navigation |
| `G then L` | Go to library | Navigation |
| `G then S` | Go to Swap Meet | Navigation |

### Editor Shortcuts
| Shortcut | Action | Context |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `Cmd/Ctrl + S` | Save/submit | In editor |
| `Cmd/Ctrl + Enter` | Save and close | In editor |
| `Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + P` | Toggle preview | In editor |

### List/Grid Shortcuts
| Shortcut | Action | Context |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `J` / `K` | Move down/up | In list |
| `Enter` | Open selected | In list |
| `S` | Save/bookmark | On item |
| `C` | Copy prompt | On item |
| `/` | Focus search | In list |

### Navigation Shortcuts
| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `[` / `]` | Previous/next page |
| `?` | Show keyboard help |

## Implementation

### Keyboard Hook
```typescript
// src/hooks/use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts
import { useHotkeys } from 'react-hotkeys-hook';

export function useKeyboardShortcuts() {
  const router = useRouter();
  
  // Global navigation
  useHotkeys('g h', () => router.push('/dashboard'));
  useHotkeys('g l', () => router.push('/library'));
  useHotkeys('g s', () => router.push('/swap-meet'));
  
  // Search
  useHotkeys('mod+k', (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    openSearchModal();
  });
  
  // Help
  useHotkeys('shift+/', () => openShortcutsHelp());
}
```

### Shortcuts Help Modal
Display all shortcuts in an organized modal:
- Grouped by context
- Show current platform keys (Cmd vs Ctrl)
- Search shortcuts

### Visual Indicators
- Show shortcut hints in tooltips
- Keyboard icon badge on buttons
- Shortcut in dropdown menu items

## Dependencies
```json
{
  "react-hotkeys-hook": "^4.0.0"
}
```

## Accessibility
- Don't override screen reader shortcuts
- Provide non-keyboard alternatives
- Announce actions to screen readers

## Files to Create
- `src/hooks/use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts`
- `src/components/keyboard-shortcuts-help.tsx`
- `src/lib/shortcuts.ts` (shortcut definitions)

## Success Criteria
- All shortcuts work as documented
- No conflicts with browser/OS shortcuts
- Help modal is discoverable
- Shortcuts work across all browsers

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","title":"Implement theme provider and dark mode toggle","description":"**Details:**
- ThemeProvider wrapping app for dark/light mode
- Persists preference to localStorage
- Respects system preference on first visit
- Dark mode toggle in Nav component
- CSS variables switch based on theme

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx
'use client';
import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useState } from 'react';

type Theme = 'light' | 'dark' | 'system';

export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [theme, setTheme] = useState<Theme>('system');
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const stored = localStorage.getItem('theme') as Theme;
    if (stored) setTheme(stored);
  }, []);
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const root = document.documentElement;
    const isDark = theme === 'dark' || 
      (theme === 'system' && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches);
    root.classList.toggle('dark', isDark);
    localStorage.setItem('theme', theme);
  }, [theme]);
  
  return (
    <ThemeContext.Provider value={{ theme, setTheme }}>
      {children}
    </ThemeContext.Provider>
  );
}
```

**Toggle Component:**
- Sun/Moon icon button
- Cycles through light → dark → system
- Tooltip showing current mode

**Location:** 
- apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx

**Reasoning:** Dark mode is expected in developer tools. System preference respect shows attention to detail. Persistent preference provides consistent UX.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:00.527000897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ThemeProvider with light/dark/system modes, ThemeToggle component, integrated into Providers.tsx. Ready for Nav component to include the toggle. Build and tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vsk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:03.111792364-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4o18","title":"Build Swap Meet browse and discovery UI","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-msj.

## Task
Build the Swap Meet (community prompts) browse and discovery UI.

## Pages

### /swap-meet (Community Browse)
Main community prompts gallery page.

Components:
- Hero section with "Swap Meet" branding and search
- Category pills for quick filtering
- Sort dropdown (Trending, Newest, Top Rated)
- Grid of PromptCard components
- Infinite scroll pagination
- "Featured" section for editor picks
- Empty state when no results

### /swap-meet/[id] (Prompt Detail)
Individual prompt view page.

Components:
- Full prompt content with syntax highlighting
- Copy to clipboard button
- Save to library button
- Fork button
- Author info with avatar and reputation
- Rating display and rate buttons
- Tags list
- Related prompts section
- Share button

## Shared Components

### PromptCard
Reusable card for displaying prompts in grids.

Props:
- prompt: PromptData
- showAuthor: boolean
- showRating: boolean
- onSave: () => void
- onRate: (value) => void

Features:
- Title and truncated description
- Category badge
- Rating indicator (approval %)
- Save/bookmark icon
- Quick copy button
- Hover preview

### CategoryFilter
Horizontal scrollable category pills.

### SortDropdown
Dropdown for sort options with icons.

### RatingButtons
Thumbs up/down buttons with counts.

## Styling
- Use existing Tailwind + Framer Motion patterns
- Card hover animations
- Smooth infinite scroll
- Mobile-responsive grid (1 col mobile, 2 tablet, 3 desktop)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-a4ot","title":"Create error pages (404, 500, maintenance)","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-c3g. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create error pages for 404, 500, and maintenance mode.

## Pages

### 404 Not Found (/not-found.tsx or app/not-found.tsx)
- Friendly headline: "Page not found"
- Subtext: "The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved."
- Search bar: "Try searching for what you need"
- Links: Home, Swap Meet, Help
- Illustration (optional)

### 500 Server Error (app/error.tsx)
- Headline: "Something went wrong"
- Subtext: "We're working on fixing this. Please try again."
- "Try again" button (reset error boundary)
- "Go home" link
- Report issue link (optional)

### Maintenance Mode
- Check for maintenance flag (env var or feature flag)
- If enabled, show maintenance page:
  - "We'll be back soon"
  - Expected duration (if known)
  - Subscribe for updates (email input)
  - Status page link

## Implementation Notes
- Next.js App Router conventions:
  - not-found.tsx for 404
  - error.tsx for runtime errors
  - global-error.tsx for root errors
- Maintenance: middleware check or layout wrapper

## Styling
- Consistent with app design
- Centered layout
- Calming colors
- Optional: Fun illustration/animation

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-a7ms","title":"Create Terms, Privacy Policy, and Guidelines pages","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-qc8.

## Task
Create legal pages for Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and Community Guidelines.

## Pages

### /terms (Terms of Service)
Key sections:
1. Acceptance of Terms
2. Description of Service
3. User Accounts
4. Subscription and Billing
5. User Content and Ownership
6. Prohibited Conduct
7. Intellectual Property
8. Disclaimer of Warranties
9. Limitation of Liability
10. Termination
11. Governing Law
12. Contact Information

### /privacy (Privacy Policy)
Key sections:
1. Information We Collect
   - Account data (email, name from Google)
   - Content you create
   - Usage data
2. How We Use Information
3. Information Sharing (Stripe, Supabase, analytics)
4. Data Retention
5. Your Rights (access, deletion, export)
6. Cookies
7. Children's Privacy
8. Changes to Policy
9. Contact

### /guidelines (Community Guidelines)
Key sections:
1. Be Respectful
2. Prohibited Content
   - Spam
   - Malware/harmful prompts
   - Copyright infringement
   - Hate speech
   - Personal information
3. Enforcement
4. Reporting
5. Appeals

## Implementation
- MDX files for easy editing
- Last updated date display
- Table of contents sidebar
- Print-friendly styling
- Version in footer (v1.0)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-akwo","title":"CLI cross-repo dependency matrix","description":"## Task
Add a CLI cross-repo dependency matrix to keep public CLI work aligned with premium APIs.

## Why This Matters
- Prevents drift between CLI client and premium backend
- Clarifies which premium endpoints power each CLI command
- Speeds up planning and reduces integration bugs

## Matrix (Public -> Premium)
- jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z (CLI login)
  - premium device code endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1
  - premium verification page: jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm
  - premium CLI auth page: jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0
  - premium JWT/token utils: jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl
  - premium refresh/revoke: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve
  - premium device_codes schema: jeffreysprompts_premium-h8r
  - premium CLI device code tests: jeffreysprompts_premium-wdao
- jeffreysprompts.com-hiff (CLI sync)
  - premium CLI sync endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o
- jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3 (CLI collections)
  - premium CLI collections endpoints (via sync): jeffreysprompts_premium-67o
- jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq (CLI offline/cache)
  - premium CLI sync endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o
- jeffreysprompts.com-t524 (CLI API docs)
  - premium CLI endpoints to document: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o, jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1, jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve, jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl
- jeffreysprompts.com-rd99 (CLI integration docs)
  - premium CLI auth + sync flows: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1, jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm, jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0, jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl, jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve, jeffreysprompts_premium-67o

## Update Rules
- When adding a new CLI command, add the premium dependency here.
